content: Clinical management during drug shortages: (1) Assess inventory and project duration; (2) Implement conservation strategies (dose optimization, alternative routes, smaller vial sizes); (3) Identify therapeutic alternatives within class or different classes; (4) Develop institutional protocols for prioritization (e.g., reserve critical drugs for most vulnerable patients); (5) Communicate with prescribers about available alternatives and dosing changes; (6) Compound preparations if commercially unavailable (following USP <797> standards); (7) Coordinate with group purchasing organizations and other institutions; (8) Document all substitutions in medical records. ASHP guidelines recommend forming multidisciplinary shortage committees. Patient safety must remain paramount; avoid using expired products unless no alternatives exist and benefits clearly outweigh risks.
